data.reasoningText: The user wants to set up hot reload for their .NET test project so they can iterate faster on failing tests. Let me first look at the project files to understand the setup.

data.content: I can see the project — the express delivery test expects `15.00m` but the method returns `10.00m`. Let me start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically when you save changes:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload. I can see that the express test expects 15.00m but the method returns 10.00m, so that's the failing test.

To set up hot reload, I just need to start `dotnet watch test` in the project directory. This will watch for file changes and re-run the tests automatically.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-f54df71d2ce443358f2ef7742771215c && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.reasoningText: There's a compilation error - `ArgumentException` needs `System` using directive. Let me fix that first, then the test will rebuild automatically.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running (shell: `watch`). It compiles and re-runs tests every time you save a file.

**Current status:** 1 passed, 1 failed — `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects `15.00` but `CalculateShipping` returns `10.00` for `"express"`.

You can now edit `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs` and the watcher will automatically re-run the tests. Would you like me to fix the failing test too?
